source.title: The Uthmanic Codex and Qur'an Compilation
source.summary: Around 650 AD (approximately 20 years after Muhammad's death), Caliph Uthman ordered the standardization of the Qur'anic text due to regional variations in recitation. He had copies made from compiled materials and sent them to major cities, ordering all other Qur'anic materials burned. This created a single standard text but also eliminated evidence of earlier variants.
